@graph.name: Mercedes-Benz models fitted with the M256 family

mainEntity.name: How much does an M256 engine replacement cost?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned M256 typically costs £3,500–£5,500 supply only [ESTIMATED], varying by model and whether the 48V system components are included. Fitting adds £650–£1,300 depending on labour rates and host vehicle.

mainEntity.name: Is the M256 a reliable engine?

acceptedAnswer.text: The M256 rates 4.0★ out of 5 based on early UK owner feedback and specialist insight [INDUSTRY ESTIMATE]. It is mechanically robust, but the 48V system and EGR cooler are areas of concern.

mainEntity.name: What is the most common M256 failure?

acceptedAnswer.text: EGR cooler coolant leaks are the most widely reported issue, sometimes leading to engine bay fires — Mercedes has issued recalls. 48V electrical glitches (ISG, battery) also surface on early cars.

mainEntity.name: Which Mercedes models use the M256?

acceptedAnswer.text: The M256 is found across the S‑Class, E‑Class, CLS, GLE, GLS, CLE, AMG GT 4‑Door, and some G‑Class models — essentially all mid‑to‑high‑end vehicles using the 3.0L straight‑six.

mainEntity.name: How long does the M256 last?

acceptedAnswer.text: With proper maintenance and recall work, the M256 is expected to exceed 150,000 miles. Long‑term data is still emerging, but its predecessor (M276) was robust, and the M256 shares similar core engineering.

hero.h1: Mercedes-Benz M254/M256/M257 Engine — Specs, Reliability & Replacement Cost
hero.subHeadline: The M256 is Mercedes' 3.0L mild-hybrid straight-six — refined, powerful, but the 48V electrical system adds complexity. Backed by real UK enquiries in 2025 [ESTIMATED].

verdictRating.confidence: 85% confidence — based on UK specialist feedback and owner reports, with limited long‑term data.

rows.score: 12 (Mix of manageable repairs and complex 48V/EGR issues)
16 (Good availability from Mercedes and some aftermarket, but specialised parts)
20 (Reconditioned cost is well under 30% of high‑value host vehicles (e.g. S‑Class, GLE))

verdictRating.oneLineVerdict: The M256 is a silky‑smooth, powerful straight‑six — the 48V system and EGR cooler are its main weak points — buy with full Mercedes service history and warranty.
verdictRating.bestFor: Buyers wanting modern refinement and efficiency with strong performance.
verdictRating.avoidIf: You're uncomfortable with potential 48V electrical or EGR cooler repairs.

costGuide.labourEstimate: Fitting an M256 typically takes 10–14 hours at £65–£95/hr (independent) or £110–£160/hr (London/SE dealer) [ESTIMATED].
